title: $1,000 in Total US Stock Market for 20 Years (2004)
description: What $1,000 invested in the Vanguard Total Stock Market ETF (VTI) on January 2, 2004 — representing ownership in essentially every publicly traded US company — would be worth today with dividends reinvested. This is the 'boring index fund' baseline.
dividend_note: VTI pays quarterly dividends that have grown over time. DRIP assumed throughout.
expense_ratio_note: VTI has one of the lowest expense ratios in the industry (0.03%). This is the cost per year as a percentage of assets. It is already reflected in the fund's NAV.
educational_note: This is the 'boring' benchmark. No stock picking, no market timing, just owning everything. ~12% annual returns over 21 years turns $1,000 into $9,500. Used as a baseline to compare individual stock picks against.
